meta.title: HELIX brief: author the governing artifacts

narration: You have a fresh repo and one sentence of intent. Step one: author the brief — the artifacts that govern every downstream decision.

prompt: Use HELIX to frame this intent: Build a CSV-to-Parquet ingest pipeline for a healthcare claims processor.

args.args: Frame intent: Build a CSV-to-Parquet ingest pipeline for a healthcare claims processor

assistant: Healthcare claims = PHI, so compliance and scale answers ripple through every downstream artifact. Four high-leverage unknowns up front before I draft the framing pass.

args.questions: [{'question': 'What is the claims data format on the input side?', ...}]

assistant: Clarifier dismissed. I'll proceed by framing with explicit assumptions called out as open questions — actionable without inventing facts. Four Frame-layer artifacts plus the top user stories.

tool_result: wrote VIS-001 (vision, north-star, out-of-scope, deferred questions)
wrote PRD-001 (scope, NFRs, compliance block, four open questions)
wrote FEAT-001 (daily batch ingest, acceptance criteria)

assistant: Framing pass complete. Six artifacts under docs/helix/01-frame/ — VIS-001, PRD-001, FEAT-001, US-001..003 — wired by depends_on in authority order. Four open questions flagged in PRD-001 (compliance posture, cloud target, volume tier, input format) govern the next step rather than getting silently defaulted. Suggested next HELIX action: resolve OQ-1 (compliance) then route to design for FEAT-001.

narration: Six artifacts, ordered by authority. The agent has a brief it can defend any code against — and the unknowns are surfaced, not invented.
